title: rubus × dolosus
intro: Rubus × dolosus, also known as the raspberry-balsam hybrid or 'Ludwig's raspberry', is a hybrid of the Rubus idaeus and Rubus laciniatus species, characterized by its unique combination of raspberry and balsam characteristics. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of tannins, flavonoids, and anthocyanins. The medicinal properties of Rubus × dolosus have been linked to various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus × dolosus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
